brief_justification: Paper A studies biochemical signaling by running Monte Carlo simulations of stochastic reaction kinetics to obtain the temporal distribution of kinase-activation events, whereas paper B solves a combinatorial optimization problem (Minimum Weight Steiner Tree) by decomposing a global connectivity constraint into local ones and applying cavity/message-passing equations from the statistical mechanics of disordered systems. The only overlap is the generic word 'statistical' plus a network setting; the driving machinery (stochastic kinetic simulation vs. cavity-method optimization) is entirely different, so no role-for-role mapping holds.
